name: Agent Trust Skill
purpose: Local pre-action gate for AI agents before installing or enabling an external skill/plugin/MCP helper/repo dependency.
invocation_examples: python3 -m agent_trust.skill check --action install_skill --source github --url https://github.com/example/pr-review-helper --requested-permission repo_read,read_env,network --warrant "summarize current PR only" --boundary "no secrets, no external upload, no credential access"
hard_stops: secrets exposure or secret value reads
validation_commands: python3 -m agent_trust.skill check --action install_skill --source github --url https://github.com/example/pr-review-helper --requested-permission repo_read,read_env,network --warrant "summarize current PR only" --boundary "no secrets, no external upload, no credential access"
contract_notes: Discovery and provenance are evidence, not authorization.
The checker classifies external skills as dependencies with authority surface, not harmless convenience files.
The manifest now points agents at the installability self-check before first use.
A deny_or_require_review verdict means stop or reduce permissions/sandbox before use.
The one_hop_install_packet gives agents the shortest manifest -> self-check -> install_skill pre-action path before using the skill.

one_hop_install_packet.purpose: One-hop install/use/verify packet for Codex, Claude, OpenClaw, Hermes, and similar agents before using Agent Trust Skill.
one_hop_install_packet.minimal_install_skill_check_command: python3 -m agent_trust.skill check --action install_skill --source github --url https://github.com/example/pr-review-helper --requested-permission repo_read,read_env,network --warrant "summarize current PR only" --boundary "no secrets, no external upload, no credential access"
expected_acceptance_signals: minimal install_skill check emits decision=deny_or_require_review or require_review for secret/network-capable external skill
